title: sedum minimum subsp. delicatum
intro: Sedum minimum subsp. delicatum, also known as White Sedum or White Stonecrop, is a low-growing, succulent plant belonging to the family Crassulaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and saponins. The health benefits of Sedum minimum subsp. delicatum include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing, as well as its ability to support the immune system and reduce the risk of infection. Herbal preparations of Sedum minimum subsp. delicatum can be made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water or oil, and are often used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including skin conditions and respiratory issues.
